beats.narration: Every film radiograph lives or dies by one number: density. Today, the two tools that measure it: densitometer and step wedge.

payload.title: FILM DENSITY
payload.sub: The number that gates every interpretation.

beats.narration: Density is a log scale. One: ten percent of the light through. Two: one percent. Three: a tenth. Four: nearly black.

payload.heading: Each density step cuts light tenfold

beats.narration: The precision tool, the densitometer: stabilized lamp, tiny aperture, photodetector, log readout. Each shift: warm up, zero, then verify on a calibrated step tablet.
The estimating tool: the step wedge comparison film. Match a patch beside your film, read its value. Close, but subjective. The densitometer stays the referee.

payload.heading: Step wedge: match a patch, read it

beats.narration: Every reading, same discipline: verify the instrument, measure the film, record the number. A density only in your head is not a record.
